prediction: Disabling alternating value embeddings while keeping the 8-layer `SSSL` optimizer setup unchanged will slightly improve `val_bpb` because the baseline's extra 16.8M value-embedding parameters are likely a short-horizon novelty tax, so a plainer stack should spend the same 300-second budget on cleaner optimization and a touch more throughput.
why_now: The only trustworthy local performance signal is the completed `codex-transcendent-fn-mar12-0755:001` baseline at `val_bpb=0.996233`, and there is still no historical run that cleanly isolates the value-embedding antithesis. This iteration creates that missing opposition before attempting a reallocation synthesis.
knowledge_selection_reason: The archived `codex-transcendent-fn-mar12-0755:001` run is still the only completed experiment that matches the incumbent axis mix closely enough to anchor decisions. No prior completed run cleanly removed value embeddings while holding the rest of the 8-layer `SSSL` recipe fixed, so there is no credible historical opposite to cite yet.
knowledge_transcendent_prediction: If the no-value-embedding negation stays competitive with `0.996233`, the next synthesis should keep `USE_VALUE_EMBEDDINGS = False` and reinvest part of the saved complexity into extra plain depth with a lower aspect ratio, instead of restoring the full novelty stack.
thesis: The strongest current thesis is the completed 8-layer `SSSL` mixed-window baseline with alternating value embeddings, which reached `val_bpb=0.996233` on the 5-minute harness.
antithesis: The strongest current antithesis is that those value embeddings are unnecessary complexity, and a plain 8-layer `SSSL` stack can match or beat the incumbent by learning more cleanly within the same short budget.
synthesis_candidate: If the no-VE ablation is competitive, test a deeper plain stack by keeping `USE_VALUE_EMBEDDINGS = False`, raising `DEPTH` to 10, and lowering `ASPECT_RATIO` to 48 so the model stays at 512 width while the removed novelty tax turns into extra ordinary depth.

active_tensions.label: Value embeddings vs simplicity
active_tensions.why_active: The incumbent spends roughly one third of its parameters on alternating value embeddings, but no completed local run has shown whether that mechanism actually buys better `val_bpb` inside a 300-second budget.

thesis.summary: Keep alternating value embeddings because the current best run used them and they may improve information flow per token.

antithesis.summary: Remove value embeddings entirely and let plain attention plus residual mixing do the work.

active_tensions.label: Capacity vs throughput
active_tensions.why_active: The incumbent already leaves substantial VRAM headroom, but its strong result may still come more from reaching 938 steps and 491.8M tokens than from its nominal parameter count.

thesis.summary: Keep the proven 8-layer scale because short-run step count may dominate modest capacity gains.

antithesis.summary: Spend some of the simplicity dividend on more ordinary depth, even if that costs throughput.

source.text:
# Deeper plain-capacity alternative
ASPECT_RATIO = 48
DEPTH = 10
USE_VALUE_EMBEDDINGS = False

active_tensions.label: Locality vs global context
active_tensions.why_active: The incumbent's `SSSL` pattern is still an untested compromise between cheaper local attention and full 2048-token coverage.

thesis.summary: Preserve the mostly local `SSSL` pattern because it keeps throughput high while retaining periodic full-context refresh.

antithesis.summary: Use full-window attention throughout because long-range coverage may matter more than local efficiency on 2048-token sequences.

active_tensions.label: Aggression vs stability
active_tensions.why_active: The incumbent uses zero warmup and aggressive Adam rates, which may be perfect for a 5-minute sprint or may be masking a cleaner optimization regime.

thesis.summary: Keep the aggressive short-horizon optimizer settings because the harness rewards fast early movement.

antithesis.summary: Trade some early speed for cleaner convergence with lower Adam rates and a short warmup.

source.text:
# Conservative schedule alternative
EMBEDDING_LR = 0.3
SCALAR_LR = 0.25
WARMUP_RATIO = 0.05
WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.1

transcendent.emergent_thought: Only after we learn whether plain attention can stay competitive should we convert the removed novelty tax into extra depth; otherwise the branch risks mixing simplification and capacity changes before the antithesis is even real.
transcendent.concrete_change: If this no-VE negation is competitive, keep `USE_VALUE_EMBEDDINGS = False`, set `DEPTH = 10`, and lower `ASPECT_RATIO` to 48 so the added layers arrive without also widening the model.
